steps.title: Use distance from zero
steps.content: The researchers claim that correlations with velocity are stronger than correlations with density. The text defines strength by distance from zero, so the sign of a correlation is not enough. The support must show velocity values farther from zero than matching density values.

steps.title: Compare matching pairs
steps.content: Choice A compares different ratings and different variables, so it does not test the researchers' claim. Choice B compares two velocity correlations and leaves density out. Choice C only describes positive and negative signs, but sign does not determine strength. The needed evidence must compare velocity against density for each rating: before the obstacle, after the obstacle, and overall. The answer is D.
